Positions and Open Topics for Master's Thesis

Current Opportunities

No open PhD / Postdoc / HiWi positions at the moment

🎓 Master’s Thesis – Open Topics

We offer research-oriented Master’s thesis projects for motivated students interested in computer vision, deep learning, and precision livestock farming.

🐄 Topic 1: Soft-Biometric Recognition for Cattle Using Deep Learning

Offered by:
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Supervisors:
Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Description:
This project focuses on developing an automated system to recognize multiple physical and behavioral attributes of cattle from images. Using deep learning, the system aims to predict traits such as:

  • Age
  • Body condition
  • Coat color
  • Posture

The research addresses challenges including fine-grained visual discrimination, robustness to lighting and occlusions, and multi-attribute modeling.
The outcome contributes to precision livestock farming by enabling scalable and accurate cattle characterization.

📩 For more details, please contact Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi.

🐄 Topic 2: Multi-View Cattle Re-Identification Using Deep Learning

Offered by:
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Supervisors:
Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Description:
This topic investigates non-invasive cattle re-identification across multiple camera views in barn environments. The goal is to match individual animals across:

  • Frontal views
  • Lateral views
  • Elevated views

The project explores deep feature extraction, metric learning, and compares CNN- and Transformer-based backbones.
This work supports automated, markerless animal monitoring in precision livestock farming.

📩 For more details, please contact Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi.

🐄 Topic 3: Long-Term Cattle Re-Identification Across Temporal Appearance Changes

Offered by:
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Supervisors:
Prof. Dr. Florian Haselbeck
Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi

Description:
This project addresses the challenge of maintaining consistent cattle identities over long periods, despite:

  • Growth
  • Seasonal coat changes
  • Body condition variations

The objective is to develop temporal-aware deep learning models that can recognize the same animal over months.
This research is essential for long-term livestock monitoring and reliable markerless identification systems.

📩 For more details, please contact Dr. Ehsan Yaghoubi.

📬 Contact

Interested students are welcome to contact us to discuss thesis topics aligned with their background and research interests.
